text.title: Methodologies Detail Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Methodologies Detail methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Detail pages MUST emit sections in the canonical order (Summary, When-to-use, When-not-to-use, Core rules, Output contract, Failure modes, Procedure, Examples, Decision tree, Templates, Scripts, Related).
rule.rationale: Inconsistent ordering forces readers to re-orient on every page.

rule.statement: Core rules table MUST contain at least 5 testable rules with id + statement + rationale + source.
rule.rationale: Fewer than 5 rules signals the methodology is under-specified.

rule.statement: Output contract MUST show one valid + one invalid example side by side.
rule.rationale: Single examples leave the schema's negative space untested.

rule.statement: Detail page MUST link to the validate-<slug>.py script when the methodology has an output contract.
rule.rationale: Without the link, readers cannot run the schema check.

rule.statement: Detail page MUST contain zero TBD, TODO, or FIXME tokens at publish time.
rule.rationale: Published TBDs reveal an unfinished methodology.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
